facts.text: Halifax was founded June 21 1749 by Governor Edward Cornwallis with 13 transports and a sloop of war.
The town was originally called Chebucto until renamed for George Montagu‑Dunk, 2nd Earl of Halifax, a founder of the settlement.
Citadel Hill, a star‑shaped fortress in downtown Halifax, now a National Historic Site, overlooks the city and was built to protect it.
Pier 21, opened as a national museum in 1999, tells the story of nearly one million immigrants who entered Halifax between 1928 and 1971.
The Halifax Public Gardens, established in 1867, are Victorian era gardens and a National Historic Site, located just a short walk from downtown.
The original grid plan of Halifax included a central military parade square called the Grand Parade, reflecting its planned military purpose.
